task_corpus_contract.duplicate_policy: Exact normalized request duplicates are forbidden across all splits; held-out near-duplicates require exclusion before labels open.
task_corpus_contract.contamination_policy: Search task text, identifiers, and gold fields against prompts, model context, source corpora, and prior outputs before each campaign; any match is reported and the task is excluded prospectively.
task_corpus_contract.difficulty_policy: Difficulty 1-5 is author-assigned for stratification only and is not evidence of actual model difficulty.
task_corpus_contract.retention_policy: Preserve frozen corpus, exclusions, failed runs, candidates, labels, evaluator outputs, costs, and dispositions append-only for the roadmap lifecycle.

held_out_label_contract.isolation_rule: Candidate generation closes and is digest-bound before the evaluator process may load held-out labels.

model_selection.drift_rule: Resolve the model through the provider models endpoint and record returned metadata before every campaign; any identity or behavior-version change forces requalification.
Exact snapshot, tokenizer, MLX runtime, prompt template, reasoning budget, final-answer reserve, and decoding contract are frozen per campaign.
Exact snapshot and tokenizer only; this arm is a mechanism floor and cannot refute claims beyond its demonstrated capability range.

inference_contract.reasoning_answer_separation: Reasoning may be retained as an unscored diagnostic when available; only the registered schema-constrained final-decision object is scored.
inference_contract.retry_policy: No outcome-aware retry. Transport retries retain the same request identity and are counted; semantic retries require a new prospectively versioned protocol.
inference_contract.upstream_drift_rule: Any model, API, tokenizer, runtime, prompt, tool, or decoding identity change invalidates comparability until sacrificial preflight reruns.

evaluator_contract.gold_or_oracle: Frozen route plus required-residual labels for bounded decision tasks, executable outcomes for code/effect tasks, and explicit abstention where neither is available.
evaluator_contract.label_isolation: Candidate artifacts close before held-out labels load.
evaluator_contract.adjudication: Disagreement routes to abstention and recorded adjudication; the candidate-generating model cannot adjudicate itself.
evaluator_contract.candidate_evaluator_contamination_contro: No evaluator prompt, gold label, or executable oracle output appears in candidate context.

statistics_policy.confirmatory_unit: held-out task with seed nested within task and model arm
statistics_policy.minimum_meaningful_effect: Frozen per campaign before tuning outcomes; default absolute rate difference 0.10 is a planning value, not a universal scientific threshold.
statistics_policy.intervals: Report Wilson intervals for bounded rates and bootstrap task-cluster intervals for paired differences; report raw denominators and per-task outcomes.
statistics_policy.precision_power: Each campaign must justify sample size from its estimand and minimum effect before held-out execution; M2 does not manufacture a universal powered denominator.
statistics_policy.multiplicity: Name primary estimands prospectively; secondary analyses are labeled exploratory and family-wise or false-discovery handling is recorded where multiple claims are tested.
statistics_policy.missingness: Preserve and classify every missing, invalid, truncated, timed-out, or excluded run; no complete-case deletion without a stated sensitivity analysis.
statistics_policy.sequential_stopping: Only preregistered adequacy, safety, futility, or budget stops; no peeking-based success stop.
statistics_policy.exploratory_boundary: Sacrificial and tuning results may repair instruments or select ranges but never enter confirmatory estimates.

environment_manifest.network_policy: disabled for local control arms; provider arms permit only the recorded provider endpoint
environment_manifest.clock_policy: monotonic clock for durations and UTC timestamps for records
environment_manifest.process_file_policy: campaign-specific temporary directory, explicit input/output allowlist, no inherited writable project paths except declared artifact destination

safety_envelope.credentials: least privilege, environment-injected, never written to artifacts
safety_envelope.sandbox: isolated repository copy or temporary workspace with synthetic/reversible effects
safety_envelope.external_actions: no unbounded browsing, messaging, publication, account mutation, spending, self-propagation, or long-lived process
safety_envelope.emergency_stop: operator interrupt plus immediate new-request stop; in-flight and completed effects remain in the ledger
safety_envelope.data_minimization: public-safe corpus only; scan candidates and artifacts for secrets, tokens, email addresses, and private paths before retention or export
safety_envelope.incident_route: stop new work, preserve evidence, revoke credentials, quarantine artifacts, inventory effects, assign residual owner, and require prospectively versioned restart

artifact_protocol.checksums: SHA-256 for every retained input, output, manifest, and derived result; derivations bind exact parent digests.
artifact_protocol.failed_run_policy: Retain failed, malformed, truncated, excluded, and stopped runs with cost and reason; never overwrite them with repaired outcomes.
artifact_protocol.redaction: Redactions create a receipt naming source digest, removed field classes, policy basis, reviewer, and public artifact digest.
artifact_protocol.reconstruction: Record environment, dependency locks, exact commands, seeds, model identities, and unavoidable nondeterminism.
artifact_protocol.public_replay: Provide one public-safe command when licenses and payload safety permit; otherwise record the exact blocking reason and a sanitized verifier packet.

activation_rule: M2 may close after this tracked foundation and its sacrificial parser/evaluator canaries validate. No outcome-bearing model campaign starts until its campaign-specific model access, sample-size, safety, and replay extensions also validate.
non_claims: The authored corpus is not proof that the tasks are representative, difficult, natural in distribution, uncontaminated in every model, or sufficient for any chapter claim.
The sacrificial canaries test parser and evaluator plumbing, not model capability, usefulness, safety, or transfer.
Selecting a current provider model does not establish access, identity resolution, performance, reproducibility, or provider-reported claims.
The statistics policy does not manufacture power; each outcome-bearing campaign still requires a prospective sample-size justification.
No publication, release, external action, support promotion, SOTA, AGI, or ASI authority follows.
